description: Close the wave-mode silent-completion gap: when a slot agent commits work with the {TASK-ID}-COMPLETED marker but the subprocess exits before flushing the <completed> tag, the task today stays in_progress and the next loop has to re-do the work. Surface slot 0's pre-merge HEAD on MergeOutcomes, add a post-merge git-log scan in git_reconcile.rs that marks matched tasks done, wire it into run_wave_iteration, and drain pending_slot_tasks so the loop-exit cleanup doesn't undo the DB write.

criteria: Rust: No warnings in `cargo check` output
Rust: No warnings in `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` output
Rust: All scoped tests pass with `cargo test -p task-mgr <module>` (REVIEW-001 runs the full suite)
No public-API breaking changes — MergeOutcomes.pre_merge_head is additive (Option<String>, defaulted via #[derive(Default)])
No new abstractions; reuse contains_task_id, prefix_and, complete_cmd::complete, update_prd_task_passes, rev_parse_head
globalAcceptanceCriteria.description: These criteria apply to ALL implementation tasks

priorityPhilosophy.description: Hierarchy of what matters most when implementing tasks
hierarchy: 1. PLAN — Anticipate edge cases before coding
2. PHASE 2 FOUNDATION — ~1 day now to save ~2+ weeks later (1:10+ ratio); we are pre-launch, foundations compound
3. FUNCTIONING CODE — Pragmatic, reliable, wired in per plan
4. CORRECTNESS — Compiles, type-checks, scoped tests pass deterministically
5. CODE QUALITY — Clean code, qualityDimensions satisfied, no warnings
principles: Quality dimensions explicit — qualityDimensions on every task tells you what 'good' looks like
Phase 2 foundation — prefer solutions that lay strong post-launch foundations (1:10+ savings ratio)
Edge cases = test cases — every known edge case must have a corresponding test
Scoped per-iteration tests, full suite at REVIEW-001 — REVIEW must leave the trunk green including pre-existing failures
Ship working code with tests to prove it; handle Option/Result explicitly; avoid unwrap() in production

prohibitedOutcomes: Tests that only assert 'no crash' or check type without verifying content
Tests that mirror implementation internals (break when refactoring)
Abstractions with only one concrete use
Error messages that don't identify what went wrong
Catch-all error handlers that swallow context
Touching reconcile_external_git_completions or its call sites — this change is additive only
Bare task-mgr init --from-json (wipes status fields); use task-mgr loop init --append --update-existing for any mid-loop JSON sync

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: MergeOutcomes struct gains `pub pre_merge_head: Option<String>` with rustdoc explaining the three None-yielding cases
Positive: `merge_slot_branches_with_resolver` sets `outcomes.pre_merge_head = rev_parse_head(slot0_path).ok()` immediately after `let slot0_path = &slot_paths[0];` at line 1875
Negative: pre_merge_head must NOT be captured before the early-return guards at lines 1856-1873; verify by reading the diff
Negative: per-slot pre_merge_head at run_slot_merge_attempt:2003 must remain unchanged
Unit test: `pre_merge_head_captured_when_num_slots_gt_1` — multi-slot call returns `Some(<40-char hex>)` in pre_merge_head
Unit test: `pre_merge_head_none_when_num_slots_le_1` — single-slot call returns None (early-return path at worktree.rs:1856-1857)
Unit test: `pre_merge_head_none_when_slot_paths_invalid` — call with slot_paths.len() < num_slots returns None (validation-failure path at worktree.rs:1859-1872)
Known-bad: if rev_parse_head used `.unwrap()`, a fresh-repo test (no initial commit) would panic; the `.ok()` form must produce None instead
CONTRACT: existing callers of merge_slot_branches_with_resolver (engine.rs:1810, reconcile_stale_ephemeral_slots) compile cleanly without changes — additive field with default
userStories.description:
Add a `pre_merge_head: Option<String>` field to `MergeOutcomes` (`src/loop_engine/worktree.rs:1595-1598`) and capture slot 0's HEAD via the existing `rev_parse_head` helper inside `merge_slot_branches_with_resolver`, immediately after the early-return guards at lines 1856-1873 succeed and `slot0_path` is bound at line 1875.

DO: Use `rev_parse_head(slot0_path).ok()` so any capture failure becomes `None` rather than aborting the merge. Document the field with rustdoc describing the three cases that yield `None` (num_slots <= 1, slot_paths validation failure, rev_parse_head Err) and that the field is consumed by `reconcile_merged_slot_completions`.

DO NOT: Touch the per-slot rev-parse at `run_slot_merge_attempt:2003` — that capture is used for the `git reset --hard` rollback target and must remain per-attempt. Do NOT change the early-return behavior; if num_slots <= 1, return immediately with default outcomes (which now includes pre_merge_head=None).

Edge cases:
- num_slots <= 1: pre_merge_head must be None (early-return path).
- slot_paths.len() < num_slots: pre_merge_head must be None (validation-failure path).
- rev_parse_head returns Err (e.g., fresh repo with no HEAD): pre_merge_head must be None; merge loop continues normally.

Known-bad to guard against:
- Capturing pre_merge_head BEFORE the early-return guards would set it to Some(hash) in cases where num_slots<=1 or slot_paths is invalid, breaking the invariant that None means 'do not run post-merge reconcile'. Capture must be AFTER both guards.
- Using `unwrap()` on rev_parse_head would panic on a fresh repo or git command failure — must use `.ok()` to convert Err into None.
edgeCases: slot_paths.len() < num_slots: pre_merge_head is None (validation-failure path)
rev_parse_head returns Err (no HEAD ref): pre_merge_head is None, merge continues

failureModes.cause: rev_parse_head fails (no HEAD, git not in PATH, IO error)
failureModes.expectedBehavior: pre_merge_head set to None; merge loop proceeds exactly as before

userStories.notes:
Reuse `rev_parse_head` at worktree.rs:1605. The existing #[derive(Default)] on MergeOutcomes means Option<String> defaults to None automatically — no constructor changes. Existing tests for `merge_slot_branches_with_resolver` are in src/loop_engine/worktree.rs mod tests (starts ~:2334). Add the three new tests adjacent to the existing ones.

Learning [1914]: Task DB vs git history can desync when a prior iteration committed but didn't sync the DB — this is exactly the bug the parent feature fixes; this task is the substrate. Learning [2788]: Slot 0 reuses the loop's branch; slots 1+ create ephemeral branches — slot_paths[0] IS the loop's primary working tree. Learning [866]: Three-file coordinated change (helper, consumer, caller) is the natural pattern for this work.
qualityDimensions: Additive struct field — no breaking change to existing callers
None-on-failure semantics consistent for all three failure paths
Rustdoc on the new field is precise about when it's None and who consumes it
Tests pin all three None cases AND the happy-path Some case
userStories.title: Surface pre-merge HEAD on MergeOutcomes

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: New function exists at git_reconcile.rs with signature `pub(crate) fn reconcile_merged_slot_completions(slot0_path: &Path, pre_merge_head: &str, conn: &mut Connection, run_id: &str, prd_path: &Path, task_prefix: Option<&str>) -> Vec<String>`
Positive: Uses `git log --no-merges {pre_merge_head}..HEAD --format=%H%x00%B%x00` exactly

Positive: Reuses `contains_task_id` (git_reconcile.rs:148), `prefix_and` (db/prefix.rs:94), `complete_cmd::complete` (commands/complete.rs:68 with force=false), `update_prd_task_passes` (prd_reconcile.rs:122)
Positive: Per-task stderr emits `Post-merge reconcile: marked {id} done (found in merged-back commits)`
Negative: Function does NOT panic on any failure path — every IO/DB/git error is warn-and-continue, return is always Vec<String>
Negative: NEVER uses force=true on complete_cmd::complete — dependency gating must remain
Negative: reconcile_external_git_completions and its call sites are unchanged
Unit test (a) happy-path: slot worktree has commit with FEAT-001-COMPLETED in body, pre→HEAD range covers it, function returns vec!["FEAT-001"], DB row is `done`, PRD `passes: true`
Unit test (b) no-match: pre→HEAD has commits but none contain the marker → returns vec![], no DB writes, no PRD writes
Unit test (c) no-op merge: pre_merge_head == HEAD (no commits in range) → returns vec![], git log emits empty stdout, no DB writes
Unit test (d) commit-body match (not subject): marker in body, not subject; function still finds it
Unit test (e) cross-PRD prefix isolation: task_prefix Some("acdfb313") does NOT let `P9-FEAT-001-COMPLETED` match `acdfb313-FEAT-001`
Unit test (f) empty pre_merge_head short-circuits: pass "", function returns vec![] immediately; pass a non-existent path to assert no git invocation happened
Unit test (g) force=false respects dep gating: task whose dependency is incomplete does NOT get marked done even when marker is present

Unit test (h) merge-commit poisoning defended: synthesize a merge commit whose body carries FEAT-001-COMPLETED (e.g., `git merge -m "Merge slot-1<newline><newline>FEAT-001-completed"`); confirm --no-merges filter excludes it and the task is NOT marked done
Unit test (i) non-UTF-8 commit body does not panic: build a commit via `git commit-tree` with invalid UTF-8 bytes in the message; assert the reconcile returns cleanly
Known-bad: if --no-merges were dropped, test (h) would fail by marking FEAT-001 done from the merge commit's body
Known-bad: if return type were usize, the call site in FEAT-003 couldn't drain pending_slot_tasks without a re-query; pin the Vec<String> contract with a test that inspects the returned IDs
userStories.description:
Add `pub(crate) fn reconcile_merged_slot_completions(slot0_path, pre_merge_head, conn, run_id, prd_path, task_prefix) -> Vec<String>` to `src/loop_engine/git_reconcile.rs`, immediately after `reconcile_external_git_completions` (after current line 143). The function scans `{pre_merge_head}..HEAD` in `slot0_path` for commits whose bodies contain `<TASK-ID>-COMPLETED` markers and marks matched tasks done.

DO: Mirror the existing `reconcile_external_git_completions` pattern (`git_reconcile.rs:23-143`) for: candidate enumeration via `prefix_and(task_prefix)` SQL; word-boundary matching via `contains_task_id`; DB write via `complete_cmd::complete(conn, &[id], Some(run_id), None, false)` with force=false; PRD update via `update_prd_task_passes(prd_path, id, true, task_prefix)`; per-task stderr line. Return `Vec<String>` of reconciled IDs (NOT usize) so the caller can drain pending_slot_tasks.

Use the git log invocation: `git log --no-merges {pre_merge_head}..HEAD --format=%H%x00%B%x00`. The `--no-merges` flag is LOAD-BEARING — it excludes merge commits produced by ClaudeMergeResolver, whose `git commit --no-edit` can carry merged-in commit bodies and would otherwise allow a resolver merge commit on slot A to mark slot B's task done. Slot agents write single-parent commits; the resolver writes merge commits.

DO NOT: Touch `reconcile_external_git_completions` or its call sites — this is additive. Do NOT use `--oneline` — slot agents put the marker in the commit body, not just the subject. Do NOT use a fixed depth scan (e.g. `-N`); the pre/post HEAD range bounds it naturally. Do NOT panic on git failure, malformed UTF-8, or DB errors — every failure path is warn-and-continue.

Edge cases:
- pre_merge_head is empty string: return Vec::new() with one stderr line (capture-failed sentinel from FEAT-001).
- pre_merge_head == HEAD (no commits in range, no-op merge): return Vec::new(); git log emits empty stdout.
- Commit body contains marker but for an already-done task: SQL filter (`status NOT IN ('done', 'irrelevant')`) excludes it; no double-marking.
- Commit body contains a marker whose task has unmet dependencies: complete_cmd::complete fails dep-gating with force=false; warn-and-skip, do not error.
- Non-UTF-8 commit body bytes: `String::from_utf8_lossy` at git_reconcile.rs:202 already handles this; pin with a test.
- Merge commit body carries `FEAT-001-COMPLETED` copied from a merged-in slot commit: `--no-merges` filter excludes it; task NOT marked.
- Cross-PRD ID collision (e.g., another PRD's P9-FEAT-001 vs this PRD's acdfb313-FEAT-001): prefix_and isolates candidate enumeration; contains_task_id's word-boundary check prevents the marker from matching.

Known-bad to guard against:
- Returning usize instead of Vec<String>: the caller (FEAT-003) needs the IDs to drain pending_slot_tasks via retain. Returning a count forces a re-query of 'just-completed-in-this-run-id', which is messier and slower.
- Omitting --no-merges: a resolver merge commit on slot 1's merge could carry slot 2's `FEAT-002-COMPLETED` marker in its body and falsely mark FEAT-002 done. The test (h) below pins this.
- Using `force=true` for complete_cmd::complete: would bypass dependency gating. Slot tasks are in_progress at this boundary (pending_slot_tasks drain only fires at loop exit), so force=false is correct AND preserves dep gating identically to the external-git reconcile.
edgeCases: Empty pre_merge_head: immediate return Vec::new() with one stderr line
Empty range (pre == HEAD): empty git log output → return Vec::new()
Already-done candidate: excluded by SQL filter; no double-marking
Unmet-dependency candidate: complete() with force=false fails dep-gating; warn-and-skip
Non-UTF-8 commit body: String::from_utf8_lossy handles; no panic
Merge commit with copied marker body: --no-merges excludes; task NOT marked
Cross-PRD prefix collision: prefix_and scopes candidates; contains_task_id word-boundary blocks marker match

failureModes.cause: git log subprocess fails (binary missing, repo corrupt)
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Warn to stderr; return Vec::new(); do not propagate

failureModes.cause: complete_cmd::complete fails for one ID (dep gating, transition violation)
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Warn to stderr; skip that ID; continue with next candidate

failureModes.cause: update_prd_task_passes fails after successful DB write
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Warn to stderr; ID still pushed into result Vec (DB write is authoritative)

userStories.notes:
Place adjacent to `reconcile_external_git_completions` so future readers see the parallel structure. Test harness: setup_test_db, setup_git_repo, insert_run in src/loop_engine/test_utils.rs (lines 69, 236, 174). Existing test module starts at git_reconcile.rs:308 — look at `test_reconcile_finds_completed_tasks_in_external_repo` for the harness shape and `test_check_git_completion_finds_task_in_commit_body` (~:498) for body-match testing pattern.

FEAT-001 must land before this task — function signature needs the `pre_merge_head` parameter and the test harness depends on the captured-HEAD semantics (though FEAT-002's tests can construct their own pre_merge_head value independently of MergeOutcomes).

Learning [2289]: `mark_task_done` at prd_reconcile.rs:192 consolidates `complete → prune crashed_last_iteration → update PRD`. NOT using it here because the external-git reconcile precedent (the model for this code) calls `complete_cmd::complete` and `update_prd_task_passes` directly and doesn't prune crashed_last_iteration. Stay consistent with the precedent unless REVIEW-001 finds a reason to consolidate. Learning [2641]: `git commit --allow-empty` is the right way to create test commits without staging files.
qualityDimensions: Mirrors reconcile_external_git_completions' control flow shape — no novel patterns
--no-merges flag is load-bearing for resolver-poisoning defense; not optional
Range-scoped (pre..HEAD), not depth-scoped — bounded by actual merged work
Returns Vec<String> not usize — enables pending_slot_tasks drain in FEAT-003
Word-boundary matching via contains_task_id prevents cross-PRD collisions
force=false on complete_cmd::complete preserves dependency gating
Every failure path is warn-and-continue; function never returns Result, never panics
userStories.title: Add reconcile_merged_slot_completions to git_reconcile

acceptanceCriteria: Positive: new `if let (Some(ref pre), Some(slot0)) = (outcomes.pre_merge_head.as_ref(), params.slot_worktree_paths.first())` block sits between line 1856 and line 1858 (BEFORE run_cmd::update and BEFORE the external-git shadow at 1869)

Positive: on non-empty reconciled Vec, agg.tasks_completed is incremented, agg.any_completed=true, ctx.crash_tracker.record_success() is called, pending_slot_tasks is retained (drain reconciled IDs), and a one-line stderr summary is emitted
Positive: import block at engine.rs:47-49 includes `reconcile_merged_slot_completions`

Positive: stale comment at engine.rs:1282-1285 rewritten to name `reconcile_merged_slot_completions` and the `{pre..HEAD}` range — verify by grep that the words 'reconcile_merged_slot_completions' appears in the rewritten comment
Negative: `params.slot_worktree_paths[0]` direct indexing must NOT appear in the new code — use `.first()`
Negative: must NOT reorder relative to the four terminal returns at lines 1891/1904/1918/1937 — they must see the incremented counter
Negative: must NOT touch the external-git reconcile block (1869-1885) — additive only

Engine-level test (added to engine.rs's existing `mod tests`, near :8476 / :8616 patterns): set up `ctx.pending_slot_tasks` with `["FEAT-001", "FEAT-002"]`, drive the wave path with FEAT-001 in the merged-back range, assert `pending_slot_tasks == ["FEAT-002"]` after the wave returns AND `agg.tasks_completed` incremented by 1 AND `agg.any_completed == true`

Known-bad: if the call were placed AFTER line 1869, the existing `let mut tasks_completed = agg.tasks_completed;` shadow would be stale and the four terminal returns would report the wrong counter. Pin with a test that asserts tasks_completed in the returned WaveOutcome includes reconciled tasks

CONTRACT: outcomes is the Vec/Struct returned by merge_slot_branches_with_resolver at line 1810; the new block reads outcomes.pre_merge_head and outcomes is still in scope at line 1857 (the existing `for (slot, detail, kind) in &outcomes.failed_slots` at line 1819 only borrows). Verify outcomes is not moved before the new block.
userStories.description:
Invoke `reconcile_merged_slot_completions` from `run_wave_iteration` in `src/loop_engine/engine.rs`, using `outcomes.pre_merge_head` (from FEAT-001) as the lower bound. Place the call between the close of the `if params.parallel_slots > 1` block at line 1856 and the `run_cmd::update` call at line 1858, BEFORE the external-git reconcile shadow at line 1869 and BEFORE all four terminal-condition gates at lines 1891, 1904, 1918, 1937.

DO: On non-empty `reconciled` Vec, (1) increment `agg.tasks_completed`, (2) set `agg.any_completed = true`, (3) call `ctx.crash_tracker.record_success()`, (4) drain reconciled IDs from `ctx.pending_slot_tasks` via `retain(|t| !reconciled.contains(t))`, (5) emit a one-line summary to stderr. Also update the import at engine.rs:47-49 to include `reconcile_merged_slot_completions`. Also rewrite the stale rustdoc at engine.rs:1282-1285 (the wave-mode `skip_git_completion_detection: true` comment) to accurately describe `reconcile_merged_slot_completions` and the `{pre..HEAD}` range — currently it falsely promises a post-merge reconcile that did not exist.

DO NOT: Place the call AFTER any terminal-condition gate — the incremented counter must be visible to all four returns. Do NOT use `[0]` indexing on `params.slot_worktree_paths`; use `.first()` and skip on None (defensive against malformed WaveParams, mirrors engine.rs:1370-1374). Do NOT skip the `pending_slot_tasks.retain` — without it, step 17.6 at engine.rs:3950 will reset the row back to `todo` at loop exit and undo the DB write done by FEAT-002.

Edge cases:
- outcomes.pre_merge_head is None (single-slot wave or capture failure): the `if let (Some(pre), Some(slot0)) = ...` block doesn't fire; existing behavior unchanged.
- params.slot_worktree_paths is empty: `.first()` returns None; reconcile skipped.
- reconciled Vec is empty (no markers found): existing behavior unchanged; no agg mutations.
- Task was already drained from pending_slot_tasks by process_slot_result (engine.rs:1436) because the slot DID flush its <completed> tag: the retain is a no-op for that ID; reconcile's DB write is also a no-op (complete_cmd returns was_already_done=true). Correct behavior.
- Two waves race for the same task: impossible by construction (run_wave_iteration is single-threaded within the loop), but document the invariant in a comment.

Known-bad to guard against:
- Placing the call AFTER the external-git reconcile block (1869-1885): the external-git block establishes a local `let mut tasks_completed = agg.tasks_completed;` shadow that the terminal returns consume. Mutating agg.tasks_completed AFTER the shadow is created leaves the shadow stale and the counter wrong in the terminal returns. Place BEFORE line 1869.
- Indexing slot_worktree_paths[0] without a .first() guard: a misconfigured WaveParams would panic mid-wave; the surrounding engine code (e.g. engine.rs:1370-1374) defends slot-path lookups this way for exactly this reason.
edgeCases: outcomes.pre_merge_head is None: block doesn't fire; existing behavior unchanged
params.slot_worktree_paths empty: .first() yields None; block doesn't fire
reconciled Vec empty: agg unchanged; no stderr noise (only emit on non-empty)
Task already drained by process_slot_result success path: retain is no-op for that ID; complete_cmd returns was_already_done=true

failureModes.expectedBehavior: agg untouched, no stderr line, terminal-condition gates evaluate unchanged

failureModes.cause: reconcile_merged_slot_completions internally fails (git error, DB error)
failureModes.expectedBehavior: Function returns Vec::new() per FEAT-002 contract; caller treats it as the no-markers case

userStories.notes:
Callers affected by modifiesBehavior: any code reading `WaveOutcome.tasks_completed` (terminal returns at engine.rs:1891/1904/1918/1937 and downstream `run_loop` accounting). The behavior change is strictly additive — `tasks_completed` can only go up by the count of reconciled IDs that weren't already counted. No caller needs to handle a NEW failure mode because the reconcile function never errors; it just returns Vec<String> (possibly empty).

Learning [866]: Three-file coordinated change (helper, consumer, caller) — this is the caller in the trio (FEAT-001=helper, FEAT-002=consumer). Learning [2728]: ctx.crashed_last_iteration is a HashMap bounded by active task count; do NOT touch it in this task (the reconcile function deliberately does not, per FEAT-002 design). Learning [2852]: Wave mode iteration pipeline shares the post-Claude post-processing with sequential — this reconcile sits OUTSIDE that pipeline (it's at the wave boundary, not the slot boundary), so it doesn't need to be in iteration_pipeline.rs.

Data contract: MergeOutcomes (worktree.rs:1595) → run_wave_iteration consumer (engine.rs:1857).
Source field (after FEAT-001): outcomes.pre_merge_head: Option<String>
Access: `if let (Some(ref pre), Some(slot0)) = (outcomes.pre_merge_head.as_ref(), params.slot_worktree_paths.first()) { ... }`
Note: outcomes is mutably borrowed at line 1819 via `for (slot, detail, kind) in &outcomes.failed_slots`; that borrow ends at the for-loop close at line 1844, so outcomes is fully available at line 1857. ConfirmAt edit time.
qualityDimensions: Injection point preserves the existing 4-terminal-return invariant — counter visible everywhere
.first() defensive pattern, not [0] indexing — mirrors engine.rs:1370-1374
pending_slot_tasks.retain is load-bearing — drains reconciled IDs so loop-exit cleanup doesn't undo the DB write
Stale comment at engine.rs:1282-1285 rewritten to accurately describe what now exists
Engine-level test pins the pending_slot_tasks drain interaction
userStories.title: Wire reconcile into run_wave_iteration + fix stale comment

acceptanceCriteria: No code duplication that could cleanly factor out (mechanical-only, not semantic)
Functions under 30 lines where reasonable (flag complex ones)
Clear separation of concerns: git_reconcile.rs owns scanning, engine.rs owns wiring, worktree.rs owns merge mechanics
Code follows existing project patterns (warn-and-continue error model, eprintln formats, rustdoc style)
Any issues found spawn REFACTOR-FIX-xxx tasks via `task-mgr add --stdin --depended-on-by REVIEW-001`
userStories.description:
Review all implementation across FEAT-001 / FEAT-002 / FEAT-003 for DRY violations, complexity, coupling, and adherence to existing patterns.

DO: Compare `reconcile_merged_slot_completions` (FEAT-002) shape-for-shape against `reconcile_external_git_completions` — they should look structurally near-identical. Verify the new function reuses contains_task_id, prefix_and, complete_cmd::complete, update_prd_task_passes. Check the engine.rs wiring (FEAT-003) for unnecessary helper extraction or branching complexity. Look for inline TODOs left behind.

DO NOT: Skip — this gate catches issues before the final review. Do NOT propose extracting a shared helper between `reconcile_external_git_completions` and `reconcile_merged_slot_completions` unless the duplication is mechanical (same SQL, same parsing) — there are real semantic differences (depth vs range, --no-merges vs not, usize vs Vec<String> return) that justify keeping them separate for now.
userStories.notes: If issues found: `echo '{...}' | task-mgr add --stdin --depended-on-by REVIEW-001` for each (priority 50-97) — atomic DB+JSON sync, no manual JSON edit. If no issues, invoke /simplify on any ugly touchpoint, then emit `<task-status>REFACTOR-001:done</task-status>` with a one-line progress note.
qualityDimensions: DRY across modules — but only when semantic equivalence holds (don't merge similar-looking but semantically different code)
Single-responsibility functions
Pattern consistency with reconcile_external_git_completions

Positive: All errors propagated with warn-and-continue (no unwrap, no expect) in the new code paths

userStories.description:
Review all implementation for quality, security, integration wiring, AND update documentation. RUN THE FULL QUALITY GATE (unscoped test suite).

DO: (1) Check every new function for error handling, check callers still work, verify no dead code, run full `cargo test`, `cargo clippy -- -D warnings`, `cargo fmt --check`. (2) Update `src/loop_engine/CLAUDE.md`: add a bullet to 'Iteration pipeline (shared)' → 'Out of scope for the pipeline' covering 'post-merge-back slot completion reconciliation'; add a new Touchpoints table row mapping 'Post-merge slot reconcile' to `git_reconcile.rs::reconcile_merged_slot_completions`. (3) Record a `task-mgr learn --outcome pattern --title 'Wave-mode slot tasks complete via post-merge reconcile when <completed> tag is dropped' --tags loop-engine,wave-mode,git-reconcile --applies-to-files src/loop_engine/engine.rs,src/loop_engine/git_reconcile.rs --confidence high` entry so future related-task `recall` surfaces this path. (4) Manually reproduce the FEAT-003 scenario per the plan's verification section (commit with `feat: <prefix>-FEAT-X-completed - test` on a slot branch, run a wave, confirm row transitions to done without the agent emitting any tag).

DO NOT: Skip the integration wiring check — unconnected code is the #1 failure mode. Do NOT scope the test suite — this is THE place the full suite runs. Do NOT skip the manual reproduction — the unit tests pin individual behaviors but not the end-to-end wave flow.
userStories.notes:
Spawn fixes via `echo '{...}' | task-mgr add --stdin --depended-on-by REVIEW-001` — DB + PRD JSON synced atomically, no manual edit. If no issues: emit `<task-status>REVIEW-001:done</task-status>` with 'Clean review' note.

Manual reproduction (run from a worktree):
1. `task-mgr init` and pick a small FEAT task to claim
2. In a slot worktree, `git commit --allow-empty -m 'feat: <prefix>-FEAT-X-completed - test'` on the slot branch
3. Let the wave run to merge-back
4. `task-mgr show <prefix>-FEAT-X` should now show `status: done` and `tasks/<prd>.json` should show `passes: true`
5. Negative: same setup but commit message WITHOUT `-completed`; row should stay `in_progress`
qualityDimensions: No unwrap in production
All new code wired to production entry point
Full suite green including pre-existing
CLAUDE.md accurately reflects the new post-merge reconcile path
task-mgr learn entry indexed so future related tasks recall this path
userStories.title: Code review + final verification + docs
